name: AAV OSK extended remaining lifespan in aged male mice
summary: Macip 2024 provides a later aged-mouse lifespan signal for AAV-mediated partial reprogramming, but it is narrow, industry-authored, and not yet human evidence.
statement: Systemically delivered AAV encoding an inducible OSK system in 124-week-old male mice extended median remaining lifespan by 109% over wild-type controls and improved frailty scores and other health parameters.
population_or_model: 124-week-old male mice treated with systemic inducible AAV OSK
time_horizon: Late-life treatment with remaining-lifespan and frailty follow-up
quantitative_note: The PubMed abstract reports a 109% increase in median remaining lifespan over controls.
caveats: This is aged-mouse evidence, not human evidence.
The study was performed by Rejuvenate Bio employees and was previously available as a preprint.
Single-sex and delivery-specific boundaries should be checked during source-fidelity review.
